items.title: Hawaii National Guard assists Waialua community with Kona Low storm preparations
items.description: Soldiers and Airmen of the Hawaii National Guard assist the community with sandbag distribution in Waialua, Hawaii, April 7, 2026. At the request of civil authorities, the Hawaii National Guard is assisting with local emergency preparations by staging vehicles and equipment statewide in preparation for heavy rains and possible flooding due to an incoming third Kona Low storm. (U.S. Army National Guard video by Spc. Daniel Barcenas)

items.title: Seasonal Allergies on the Rise: What You Need to Know and How Munson Can Help
items.description: FORT LEAVENWORTH, Kansas -- As spring arrives, some beneficiaries may begin to experience the familiar symptoms of seasonal allergies—sneezing, congestion, itchy eyes, and runny nose. While often mild, these symptoms can impact sleep, focus, and overall well-being if left unmanaged.

items.title: Winn leaders, ABA experts join forces to improve access to care for autistic military children
items.description:
Leaders from Winn Army Community Hospital, behavioral therapists and Humana TRICARE representatives joined forces March 31 to improve access to care for military families coping with autism.

The 23 Applied Behavior Analysis professionals who attended were from network mental health care providers across coastal Georgia. Gathered with them in Winn’s Patriot Auditorium were the hospital’s primary care, pediatric, mental health and Exceptional Family Member Program leads.

SANTA RITA, Guam — The crew of the USCGC Oliver Henry (WPC 1140) returned to Guam on March 29, closing out a 29-day patrol period that restored a critical tsunami early warning station on a remote Pacific atoll, delivered humanitarian supplies to two island communities, and enforced fisheries laws across more than 4,000 nautical miles.

items.title: Bayne-Jones Army Community Hospital Pharmacy Announces Temporary Closure for Administrative Functions
items.description:
FORT POLK, La. – The Department of Pharmacy at Bayne-Jones Army Community Hospital will temporarily close on Wednesday, April 8, at 2:00 p.m. to conduct administrative and inventory operations. The closure is essential for maintaining the highest standards of patient care and operational readiness.

The BJACH Pharmacy will resume normal hours, reopening at 8:00 a.m. on Thursday, April 9.

items.title: Operation Spring Clean Strengthens Warfighter Readiness Through Logistics Reset in Cairo
items.description: CAIRO — Staff from Naval Medical Research Unit (NAMRU) EURAFCENT’s logistics department recently concluded Operation Spring Clean, a mission-essential logistics reset aimed at improving accountability, efficiency and readiness at a critical supply hub supporting both United States Africa (AFRICOM) Command and United States Central Command (CENTCOM).

items.title: Shipyard spotlight: Faith Kinnaird, Code 135.1 Resource Manager, Apprentice Supervisor and Quality Insurance Instructor
items.description: Faith Kinnaird has worked in Norfolk Naval Shipyard’s (NNSY) Code 135 Non-Destructive Test (NDT) Division for 13 years. She began her NNSY career as a Metals Inspector Apprentice, transitioned to training new NDT employees and is currently a Code 135 Resource Manager, Apprentice Supervisor and Quality Insurance Instructor. She has an adventurous spirit away from work and enjoys experiencing new foods, cultures, architecture and nature.

items.title: Norfolk Naval Shipyard Holds Orientation Day With State and Local Partners Supporting Emergency Response
items.description: Norfolk Naval Shipyard (NNSY) welcomed nearly 60 representatives from various federal, state and local agencies for its Emergency Response Orientation Day March 4.

items.title: Barge Maintenance and Environmental Workboat Crew (Code 900F.44) Keeps Norfolk Naval Shipyard’s Mission Afloat
items.description: Norfolk Naval Shipyard (NNSY) has had a Barge Maintenance and Environmental Workboat Crew (Code 900F.44) for 30 years. They are responsible for emergency response to various waterborne spills and retrieving debris from Norfolk Naval Shipyard's four miles of shoreline. Additionally, they aid production efforts by getting barges into the water.

items.title: NMR&D Junior Officer of the Year Lt. Cmdr. Dawn Weir: Overseeing Military Health from Malaysia to Maryland
items.description: The Sailors of Navy Medicine Research & Development (NMR&D) seek solutions to safeguard service member health from every corner of the globe. Their efforts span eight commands, six continents and over a dozen partner nations.

items.title: Understanding the TRICARE claims process
items.description: When you receive a service, medication, or treatment as part of your TRICARE benefit, filing a claim is the way your provider gets reimbursed for their services. If you pay for services up front, filing a claim is how you get reimbursed.

items.title: Winn, TRICARE, local ABA experts gather to build stronger support network for military families
items.description:
Winn Army Community Hospital recently hosted 20+ local ABA experts and Humana TRICARE East to make it easier for our families to get the care they need.

By bringing together our pediatric and EFMP teams with community partners, we’re streamlining the autism care process. 3rd ID Command Sgt. Maj. Donald Durgin joined the talk, stressing that when our families are supported, our Soldiers can stay focused on the mission.

items.description: Meet Senior Chief Hull Maintenance Technician (HTCS) John Parkin, a Recruit Division Commander (RDC) serving at Recruit Training Command (RTC) Great Lakes, the Navy’s only boot camp.
